page.title: Marbella Yacht Charter + Malaga Airport Transfer Guide

data.meta_description: Plan your marbella yacht charter airport transfer with ease. Puerto Banús is 45 km from Málaga AGP. Book a private taxi or our 12-pax Mangusta 80.
data.summary: Step-by-step guide to combining a Marbella yacht charter with an airport transfer from Málaga (AGP). Includes distances, transfer options, and fleet recommendations for a seamless luxury day.
key_facts: Málaga Airport (AGP) to Puerto Banús: 45 km / 40 min by car
Private taxi from AGP to Puerto Banús: €65–€90 one-way
Shared shuttle: €15–€25 per person, 45–60 min journey
Rent a car from AGP from €30/day; parking at Puerto Banús €20–€30/day
Our fleet departs Puerto Banús; all charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks & 21% VAT
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) include a free jet ski; 4-hour minimum €4,719
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) from €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours

faq.q: How far is Málaga Airport from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP) is 45 km west of Puerto Banús. By car or taxi the journey takes 35–50 minutes depending on traffic. The route follows the A-7 coastal highway with views of the Mediterranean.

faq.q: What is the best way to get from Málaga Airport to my yacht charter?
faq.a: A private taxi or pre-booked transfer is the most reliable option, costing €65–€90. For groups of 4+, it's often cheaper than a shared shuttle (€15–€25 per person). Rental cars are available but parking in Puerto Banús costs €20–€30 per day.

faq.q: Can I book a transfer that includes a stop for groceries or drinks?
faq.a: Yes, private taxi drivers usually accommodate a brief stop at a supermarket or local shop. Let us know in advance and we can coordinate the timing so you arrive at the marina with everything you need.

faq.q: Do you offer airport pickup as part of the yacht charter?
faq.a: We do not include airport transfers in our standard charter packages. However, we can recommend trusted local taxi companies. You can also arrange a private transfer via the WhatsApp number on our site.

faq.q: What is the earliest time I can start a yacht charter after landing?
faq.a: Most charters begin at 10:00 or 12:00 from Puerto Banús. If your flight lands by 09:00, a 40-minute transfer gets you to the marina comfortably. For afternoon arrivals, 15:00 or 17:00 departures are also available.

faq.q: Are there luggage storage facilities at Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Puerto Banús has limited luggage storage. Most yachts have small cabins or storage areas where you can leave bags during the charter. For larger suitcases, consider leaving them at your hotel or using a left-luggage service near the port.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for a full day after a late flight?
faq.a: Yes, we offer 8-hour charters starting as late as 14:00. For example, the Astondoa 40 (€2,299) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (pricing on request) can depart at 14:00 and return at 22:00, giving you sunset cruising time.

faq.q: Do I need a visa or special documentation to charter a yacht in Spain?
faq.a: No special documentation is required for a standard charter. A valid passport or EU ID is enough. The yacht is operated by our licensed skipper. Non-EU visitors should ensure their passport is stamped upon entry to Spain.

data.body_html:
Landing at Málaga Airport (AGP) and heading straight to a Marbella yacht charter is one of the smoothest transitions on the Costa del Sol. The airport is just 45 km from Puerto Banús — roughly a 40-minute drive. This guide covers every transfer option, from private taxis to rental cars, plus how to match your arrival time with the right boat from our fleet.

Málaga Airport to Puerto Banús: Distance & Time

Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP) is the main gateway for the Costa del Sol. From the arrivals hall to the marina at Puerto Banús, the distance is exactly 45 km. By car, the journey takes 35–50 minutes depending on traffic. The route follows the A-7 motorway along the coast, passing through Torremolinos, Benalmádena, and Fuengirola. Peak season (June–September) can add 10–15 minutes, especially on Friday afternoons and Sunday evenings.

Transfer Options: Cost & Convenience

  • Private taxi: €65–€90 one-way. Pre-book via Welcome Pickups or local companies. Door-to-door, no waiting.
  • Shared shuttle: €15–€25 per person. Companies like ALSA or Suntransfers. 45–60 min journey. Good for solo travellers.
  • Rental car: From €30/day with Avis or Hertz at AGP. Parking at Puerto Banús costs €20–€30/day. Useful if you plan to explore Marbella after the charter.
  • Train + bus: Not recommended. Cercanías train goes to Fuengirola (€6), then a bus to Marbella (€3). Total 1.5–2 hours.

Best Boats for a Same-Day Arrival Charter

If you land in the morning (09:00–11:00), you have time for a full-day charter. Our Tier A boats — the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — cost €749 for 2 hours up to €2,299 for 8 hours. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

For a larger group (up to 12 pax), the Mangusta 80 (24m) is our flagship. It requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and includes a free jet ski. Three variants are available: grey, white, and the standard model. All depart from Puerto Banús.

If you arrive in the afternoon (14:00–16:00), consider a sunset charter on the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (12m, 10 pax). Pricing for these is available on request via WhatsApp.

What to Do with Your Luggage

Most of our yachts have small cabins or storage compartments where you can leave bags during the charter. For large suitcases, we recommend leaving them at your hotel or using a left-luggage service near the port. Puerto Banús has several luggage storage points, including Locker Marbella on Calle Ribera, about 5 minutes from our dock. Alternatively, ask your taxi driver to drop your bags at your accommodation before heading to the marina.

Local Tips for a Seamless Day

  • Book your taxi 24–48 hours in advance during high season (July–August).
  • Arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure to meet your skipper and stow belongings.
  • If you want to stock up on extra snacks or drinks, ask your driver for a short stop at a Mercadona or Carrefour.
  • After the charter, explore the Golden Mile or dine at one of the chiringuitos on the beach — many are a short walk from Puerto Banús.

Frequently asked questions

How far is Málaga Airport from Puerto Banús?

Málaga-Costa del Sol Airport (AGP) is 45 km west of Puerto Banús. By car or taxi the journey takes 35–50 minutes depending on traffic. The route follows the A-7 coastal highway with views of the Mediterranean.

What is the best way to get from Málaga Airport to my yacht charter?

A private taxi or pre-booked transfer is the most reliable option, costing €65–€90. For groups of 4+, it's often cheaper than a shared shuttle (€15–€25 per person). Rental cars are available but parking in Puerto Banús costs €20–€30 per day.

Can I book a transfer that includes a stop for groceries or drinks?

Yes, private taxi drivers usually accommodate a brief stop at a supermarket or local shop. Let us know in advance and we can coordinate the timing so you arrive at the marina with everything you need.

Do you offer airport pickup as part of the yacht charter?

We do not include airport transfers in our standard charter packages. However, we can recommend trusted local taxi companies. You can also arrange a private transfer via the WhatsApp number on our site.

What is the earliest time I can start a yacht charter after landing?

Most charters begin at 10:00 or 12:00 from Puerto Banús. If your flight lands by 09:00, a 40-minute transfer gets you to the marina comfortably. For afternoon arrivals, 15:00 or 17:00 departures are also available.

Are there luggage storage facilities at Puerto Banús?

Puerto Banús has limited luggage storage. Most yachts have small cabins or storage areas where you can leave bags during the charter. For larger suitcases, consider leaving them at your hotel or using a left-luggage service near the port.

Can I charter a yacht for a full day after a late flight?

Yes, we offer 8-hour charters starting as late as 14:00. For example, the Astondoa 40 (€2,299) or Azimut 58 Flybridge (pricing on request) can depart at 14:00 and return at 22:00, giving you sunset cruising time.

Do I need a visa or special documentation to charter a yacht in Spain?

No special documentation is required for a standard charter. A valid passport or EU ID is enough. The yacht is operated by our licensed skipper. Non-EU visitors should ensure their passport is stamped upon entry to Spain.
